dental-visit.description: Visit lifecycle, treatment, and chart rules

rules.description: A patient cannot have two active visits simultaneously. Enforced globally per patient (across all branches) via a partial unique index on (patient_id, status) WHERE status='active' plus the findActiveByPatient app guard — stricter than the original 'same branch' wording.
rules.test: dental-visit.test.ts (409 ACTIVE_VISIT_EXISTS on create + on activating a second visit)

rules.description: Visit state transitions are strictly linear: draft → active → completed → locked. No state reversal is permitted.
A visit is immutable after status reaches completed or locked. Chart entries, treatments, prescriptions, and lab orders cannot be added, edited, or deleted.

An empty visit (no treatments, no SOAP content, no attachments) auto-discards instead of completing. Gated behind the default-OFF feature flag dental_visit_auto_discard (V-VIS-004, ADR-010): when OFF (default) empty visits complete normally; when ON, a complete redirects to discarded.
rules.notes: Status corrected not-implemented→implemented-flag-gated (TRACEABILITY 2026-06-08). Default OFF; the discarded state + VISIT_TRANSITIONS active→discarded exist in schema.

rules.description: Treatment state transitions are forward-only: diagnosed → planned → performed → verified. dismissed is reachable from any non-terminal state. No reversal is permitted.
rules.notes: Forward-only enforcement + dismissed-from-any-non-terminal + reversal rejection are tested in treatment.fsm.property.test.ts, dental-visit.treatment-status-transitions.test.ts, and treatment-fsm-http.test.ts (illegal transitions assert 4xx). Status corrected partial→implemented (TRACEABILITY 2026-06-08).

rules.description: A performed/verified treatment is field-immutable — its procedure code, tooth, surface, and price cannot be changed (status transitions remain allowed).
Carry-over creates new dental_treatment rows on the next visit with carriedOver=true, sourceVisitId=<prior visit>, status preserved; not billed until status moves to performed.
rules.test: dental-visit.treatment-templates.test.ts (FR1.11 carries over pending; restores dismissed; 422 on completed visit)

rules.description: Applying a treatment template (POST /visits/{id}/apply-template/{tid}) creates billable treatments and is gated on the clinical role (dentist_owner/dentist_associate), parity with createDentalTreatment; the template must belong to the visit's branch (foreign-branch template → 404, no cross-clinic leak).
rules.notes: Fixed audit 2026-06-08: assertBranchAccess (any member) → assertBranchRole + branch-match guard.

rules.description: Treatment-plan read/accept/version endpoints (GET/POST /patients/{id}/treatment-plan*) authorize against the PATIENT's branch (preferredBranchId via assertPatientBranchAccess), never the caller-supplied branchId query param. A foreign-branch caller passing their own branchId cannot read or snapshot another branch's patient plan.
rules.test: dental-visit.cross-tenant-rbac.test.ts (foreign-branch owner → 403 on plan/accept/version; patient-branch owner → 200)
rules.notes: Fixed audit 2026-06-08 (V-VIS-011): cross-tenant PHI leak — branchId query param was access-checked but never tied to the patient. Mirrors V-PAT-002.

rules.description: An appointment check-in creates a visit record. Deleting the appointment does not delete the visit.

rules.description: All appointment operations are scoped to a branch (assertBranchRole/assertBranchAccess); a principal with no membership in the target branch is rejected 403.
Walk-in appointments (walkIn=true) bypass the configured working-hours validation.
Cancellation requires a reason (DELETE query param min:5/max:500) -> 422 REASON_REQUIRED when missing/blank.
Non-walk-in appointments must fall within the branch's configured working hours (timezone-aware) -> 422 OUTSIDE_WORKING_HOURS.
Double-booking: soft-warn at create (201 + warnings:[DOUBLE_BOOKING]); hard-block at reschedule (409 RESCHEDULE_CONFLICT). No DB unique constraint (intentional).

An invoice requires at least one performed/verified treatment. Creating an invoice with zero billable treatments → 422 NO_BILLABLE_TREATMENTS. A treatment already billed on a prior invoice → 422 TREATMENT_ALREADY_BILLED (double-billing prevention, S1-T7).

rules.description: An active payment plan blocks invoice archival (void or uncollectible) → 409 ACTIVE_PAYMENT_PLAN. Staff must resolve the plan before voiding/writing off.

Invoice state lifecycle: draft → issued → paid / partial / overdue / voided. Payments are valid only on issued/partial/overdue (draft → 422 INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION); payment on paid/voided → 422 INVOICE_IMMUTABLE; overpayment → 422 PAYMENT_EXCEEDS_BALANCE.
rules.notes: Registry reconciled 2026-06-08: legacy 'sent' state renamed 'issued' (V-BIL-015); the enum/code/contract all use 'issued'.

rules.description: Dental invoices support an 'uncollectible' terminal status (write-off), reachable from issued/partial/overdue; uncollectible is terminal (no further transitions). Owner-only.

rules.notes: Covered by invoice.fsm.property.test.ts (uncollectible reachability + terminality) and dental-billing.test.ts. Registry reconciled 2026-06-05: prior 'deferred' status was stale.

rules.description: A signed consent form is required before invoicing a visit → 422 CONSENT_REQUIRED (V-BIL-007; previously mislabeled BR-011 in code).

Money-integrity bounds (V-BIL-001/002/010): discount percentageRate must be 0–100 (else 422 INVALID_DISCOUNT_RATE — an out-of-range rate drives totals negative); installment count must be 2–24 (else 422 INVALID_INSTALLMENT_COUNT — guards division-by-zero); payment amountCents ≥ 1 (else 422 INVALID_AMOUNT). Each is enforced in the handler as defense-in-depth even if the schema validator is bypassed.

Cross-tenant report scoping: the five billing report/list endpoints whose branchId filter is OPTIONAL (AR aging, collections summary, payer aging, claim worklist, statement batch) MUST scope results to the caller's own active branches when branchId is omitted — NEVER the whole multi-tenant DB. Omitting branchId previously aggregated every org's invoices/payments/claims/balances + patient PHI.

rules.description: An invoice's dueDate derives from payment terms (in days) at issue, UNLESS the caller supplied an explicit dueDate at create (that wins). Precedence: per-invoice override (paymentTermsDays) → MAX of the line-item service terms (dental_procedure_code.paymentTermsDays keyed by line cdtCode) → clinic default (branch settings defaultPaymentTermsDays) → 0. dueDate = issuedAt + resolvedDays. Terms are bounded 0–365; Net-0 = due on receipt.

rules.description: The daily status sweep (cron dental-billing.status-sweep, 02:00 UTC) auto-transitions issued/partial invoices to `overdue` where dueDate <= now AND balanceCents > 0. Each transition is audited (action 'invoice.overdue', actor = system BILLING_SYSTEM_ACTOR, targetType dental_invoice). Idempotent: the status filter excludes already-overdue/paid/voided rows, so a re-run flips nothing and writes no further audit row. Terminal (voided/paid/uncollectible) invoices are never touched.

rules.description: Dunning: after the daily overdue sweep, every currently-overdue invoice (status overdue, balanceCents > 0, patient has a person record) fires a payment reminder at each configured days-past-due offset that has elapsed. Cadence = branch settings billingReminderOffsetDays, default [3,7,14]. Channels = email + push (SMS deferred — notifs 'sms' is a no-op). Idempotency: a dental_billing_reminder_log row is CLAIMED per (invoiceId, offsetDay) via the unique index before enqueuing, so each offset sends exactly once; a total dispatch failure releases the claim so the next sweep retries. Paid/voided invoices (not status overdue) are never reminded.

rules.description: Patient credit ledger: dental_patient_credit is an append-only signed ledger (positive adds credit, negative consumes it); a patient's available credit = SUM(amountCents), never negative. applyCreditToInvoice draws credit against an invoice ATOMICALLY (withTenantTx): the available-credit read, the invoice paid/balance update (DentalInvoiceRepository.addPayment — same primitive as a cash payment), and the consuming (negative) ledger row commit together. Apply amount must be > 0, <= invoice.balanceCents, AND <= available credit (BR-052), else 422 with NO mutation (atomic rollback). Voided/settled (balance 0) invoices reject. Both add-credit and apply are audited (patient_credit.added, invoice.credit_applied).

rules.description: Payment refund (distinct from same-day void): POST /dental/billing/payments/:id/refund refunds a previously-recorded payment, partial or full. OWNER-ONLY (dentist_owner), reason-required. ATOMIC (withTenantTx): the refunded amount is reversed from the invoice (DentalInvoiceRepository.removePayment — paidCents down, balance up, status re-opened) and a dental_payment_refund record is written; when bookAsCredit, a positive patient-credit row (source 'refund') is also written (refund-to-credit, 4.1) instead of cash-out. BR-053: payment must be non-void; invoice must not be voided; amount 1 .. (payment.amountCents − already-refunded) — multi-refunds are capped at the un-refunded remainder, over-cap → 422 with no mutation. The synchronous 'payment.refunded' audit row IS the emitted event (ADR-006: no event bus).

rules.description: Invoice tax is derived from the branch tax mode (PH), NEVER from the caller (resolves BR-010 / EM-BILL-001). Branch setting taxMode: 'non_vat' (default) → taxCents 0, total = subtotal; 'vat_registered' → 12% VAT (vatRate, default 12) carved OUT of the VAT-INCLUSIVE gross subtotal so total stays = subtotal and the VAT portion is reported in taxCents. Pure carve math: vat = round(subtotal * rate / (100 + rate)). Settable via the branch-settings PUT (free-form JSONB) and the Settings → Tax (VAT) panel.

dental-clinical.description: Consent, prescription, and lab order rules

rules.description: Consent form is immutable once signed. No edits, no re-signing (422 CONSENT_FORM_SIGNED), no deletion. The signed record is append-only. signed and revoked are mutually-exclusive terminal states: a signed form cannot be revoked (422 CONSENT_ALREADY_SIGNED) and — symmetrically — a revoked form cannot be signed (422 CONSENT_FORM_REVOKED, V-CLN-010).
rules.source: dental-clinical handlers (signConsentForm.ts, revokeConsentForm.ts, consent-form.repo.ts) + consent-sheet.tsx || TEST: services/api-ts/src/handlers/dental-clinical/clinical-consent-lab.test.ts:434

rules.description: Patient registration requires explicit consent (consentGiven: true). Registration is rejected without it.
Prescription creation requires a prescriberMemberId — the ID of a dentist-role member. Non-dentist staff cannot write prescriptions.

Lab order state lifecycle: ordered → in_fabrication → delivered → fitted / cancelled. A terminal order cannot transition to any other state; the `delivered` transition is the LabOrderCompleted (DE-015) marker. (Description corrected from stale ordered→in_progress→completed wording — TRACEABILITY 2026-06-08.)
rules.source: dental-clinical handlers — lab-orders/updateLabOrder.ts; tested in clinical-consent-lab.test.ts

rules.description: A revoked consent form can never become signed (symmetric with the signed→revoke guard). Enforced in three layers: signConsentForm rejects existing.revoked → 422 CONSENT_FORM_REVOKED; ConsentFormRepository.sign() adds revoked=false to its WHERE (race-safe); and the treatment/billing consent gate hasSignedConsentForVisit matches signed=true AND revoked=false so a revoked consent never satisfies the treatment status=performed or billing guard (WF-035: patient revokes → treatment blocked). Closes a consent-integrity hole where revoke-then-sign produced a signed+revoked row that the signed-only gate would have honored.
Branch membership is required for all clinical data access. Every handler calls assertBranchAccess which verifies the requesting user holds a DentalMembership record for the requested branch.

PIN-based local auth lockout: after repeated failed verify-pin attempts the membership is locked (429 with lockedUntil). Lockout escalates from a short delay at 5 attempts to a 5-minute lockout at 10 attempts; a successful verify resets the failed-attempt counter.

Patient registration requires explicit consent (consentGiven: true), else 422 CONSENT_REQUIRED. Single-consent model (V-PAT-004): registration captures ONE consent flag persisted as a JSONB consent object { registrationConsent, capturedAt } on the person record — NOT the legacy 4-flag marketing/data_sharing/sms/email split (never implemented, removed). Per-channel communication consent is a separate endpoint (P1-28).
An archived patient record is read-only. Write operations (update demographics, add follow-up note, create recall) return 403 PATIENT_ARCHIVED.
rules.notes: Enforced on the demographics/follow-up/recall write paths; sub-resource handlers (insurance/contacts/alerts/tasks/household) do not carry the archived-write guard — tracked test/impl backlog.

rules.description: Follow-up notes are append-only — only GET (list) and POST (add) routes exist; there is no PATCH/DELETE route, so edits/deletes cannot occur.

rules.description: Completing one treatment-plan item must NOT complete the whole plan unless ALL items are complete. Plan status is derived from linked treatments: all done→completed, some done→partially_completed, none→approved; dismissed/declined treatments are excluded from the denominator.

dental-pmd.description: Portable Medical Document (canonical expansion; V-PMD-009) rules — per-visit immutable signed snapshots, external PMD import + immutability, checksum integrity, identity binding, patient-self access, continuity-of-care export.

rules.description: A PMD is an immutable per-visit snapshot generated ONLY from a completed or locked visit (otherwise 422 VISIT_NOT_COMPLETED). Content is SHA-256 checksummed (sha256-<hex>) and sealed; future changes to the source visit do NOT alter the PMD. Re-generation supersedes the prior row (generated→superseded) and never mutates a superseded row's content/checksum.

rules.description: An imported external PMD is stored verbatim and is READ-ONLY after creation. PATCH/PUT/DELETE on /dental/pmd/imported/:id are rejected at the route level with 405 IMPORTED_PMD_IMMUTABLE (not 403 — there is no GET-only generated mutate route; the guard lives in app.ts). Its data is never auto-merged into the patient's editable records; the Safety Floor merge is add-only and never mutates imported content.

A body.patientId disagreeing with visit.patientId → 422 PATIENT_VISIT_MISMATCH; the checksum-sealed content always carries visit.patientId so no client-controllable id can leak into a non-repudiation record.

dental-perio.description: Periodontal charting: one-chart-per-visit, FDI/depth validation, role gate, visit-lock cascade, AAP/EFP 2017 staging/grading

rules.description: One perio chart per visit. A second create for the same visitId returns 409 CHART_EXISTS.

Perio chart is immutable once the parent visit is completed/locked. Writes (create/upsert/complete) on a sealed visit are rejected: create→422 VISIT_LOCKED, upsert→422 VISIT_IMMUTABLE, complete→422 VISIT_LOCKED. A completed/locked chart itself rejects further writes as a 409 CHART_COMPLETED state conflict (N-PER-01).
Probing depths must be integers 0-20mm per site; recession -5..20mm. Out of range → 422 INVALID_DEPTH.

FDI tooth number must fall in a valid quadrant range (adult 11-18/21-28/31-38/41-48; primary 51-55/61-65/71-75/81-85). Cross-quadrant gaps (e.g. 19) → 422 INVALID_TOOTH_NUMBER. The TypeSpec @minValue/@maxValue only bounds 11-85; the precise disjoint check is in the handler.

Chart create/upsert/complete require a clinical role (dentist_owner, dentist_associate, hygienist) on the chart's branch. Non-clinical branch members (staff_scheduling/staff_full) and non-members → 403. Read (get/list) additionally allows staff_full; staff_scheduling is excluded from all perio access (EF-PER-002).

Tooth-reading upsert is idempotent on (chartId, toothNumber). A single-site PATCH writes only the columns present in the request (one atomic onConflictDoUpdate); other sites on the tooth are never replaced to null. No 409 on duplicate.
Completing a chart requires a minimum reading count: adult 16, primary (FDI 51-85) 8 (dentition inferred from charted tooth numbers). Below the minimum → 422 INSUFFICIENT_READINGS.

Visit-lock → chart-lock cascade. The chart `locked` state is materialized lazily on the next perio read/write: when the parent visit is completed/locked, a not-yet-locked chart transitions to `locked` and a perio.chart.locked audit marker is written (ADR-006: audit-log-only, no event bus).

On completion the chart is classified per the 2017 AAP/EFP system (stage from worst interdental CAL + complexity factors; grade from %bone-loss/age + smoking/diabetes modifiers; extent). Stage/extent are null without evidence; grade defaults to B. IDEAL-§343: remainingTeeth is NOT inferred from the charted-tooth count — a partial chart of a fully-dentate patient is not over-staged to IV via the <20-teeth factor.
rules.source: utils/perio-staging.ts; utils/perio-classify-chart.ts:118; completePerioChart.ts:126; perio-staging.test.ts; perio-classify-chart.test.ts (IDEAL-§343 case)

rules.description: An image must be linked to a patient (patient_id NOT NULL). It may optionally be linked to a visit and/or specific teeth via join table.
Image deletion is default-deny. Only dentist and associate roles may delete images. All other roles receive 403.
An associate may only delete images they personally acquired (acquiredBy === user.id). Attempting to delete another user's image returns 403.
Image deletion is soft-only. DELETE sets status='archived'; no row is physically removed.
Branch isolation applies to all 13 imaging endpoints. Every handler calls assertBranchAccess. Finding details are hidden from users without branch membership.
Legacy dental_attachment records are surfaced as imaging items via a union adapter in listPatientImages. Document and deleted attachments are excluded.
Images are cached locally in IndexedDB for offline access in the imaging workspace.

rules.description: Ceph landmark status transitions are strictly forward: placed → confirmed → locked. locked is terminal — no further status changes are permitted.
A locked landmark is fully immutable. Any PATCH to coordinates or status, or DELETE, returns 422 with code LANDMARK_LOCKED.
Invalid landmark status transitions (e.g., confirmed → placed, locked → confirmed) are rejected with 422 and code INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION.
Ceph report generation is gated on four required landmarks (A, B, Go, Po) all having status confirmed. Attempting to generate a report without all four confirmed returns a validation error.
Branch isolation applies to all ceph endpoints. A non-member requesting any CephMgmt_* endpoint receives 404 (not 403) to avoid leaking branch existence.
Ceph reports are immutable versioned snapshots. Each report has a monotonically incrementing version per image (1, 2, 3…). No update or delete handlers exist.
Every write handler (batchUpsertCephLandmarks, updateCephLandmark) triggers an inline analysis recompute and returns { items, analysis }. Analysis is always fresh after any landmark write.

analysisType is selected by the ?analysisType= query param and defaults to 'steiner_hybrid_sn'. Six protocols ship (steiner_hybrid_sn, ricketts, downs, tweed, mcnamara, jarabak); an unknown value is rejected 422 UNSUPPORTED_ANALYSIS_TYPE (never silently defaulted — showing the wrong analysis is a safety bug). The response echoes the requested analysisType.
rules.notes: Supersedes the prior v1.4 'only steiner_hybrid_sn' claim — multi-analysis shipped.

rules.description: createCephReport returns 201 with { id, imageId, version, snapshot, createdAt }. The snapshot is a frozen JSON blob containing all measurement data at creation time and never changes afterwards.
Version numbers for ceph reports are monotonically increasing per image (1, 2, 3…). A DB unique constraint on (imageId, version) prevents duplicate versions.

rules.description: updateCephLandmark (PATCH) returns { items, analysis } — the full landmark list plus recomputed analysis, not just the patched landmark.
A locked landmark is immutable across all three mutation axes: coordinate PATCH → 422 LANDMARK_LOCKED; status PATCH → 422 LANDMARK_LOCKED; DELETE → 422 LANDMARK_LOCKED.
recomputeCephAnalysis (POST /analysis/recompute) returns a single CephAnalysis object — not { items, analysis }. The endpoint is idempotent.

Ceph landmark batch upsert is idempotent — upserting the same landmark id with new coordinates replaces the existing entry without creating a duplicate row. Upsert semantics are enforced at the DB layer via onConflictDoUpdate.

A batch upsert of landmarks is rejected in its entirety if any landmark in the batch is locked. The handler skips locked landmarks silently (no partial application, no error). This protects locked entries without requiring the caller to pre-filter.

dental-audit.description: Compliance audit trail (table: dental_audit_log) — append-only, never deleted (HIPAA 7-year retention). No PHI in the log body: actor/target are UUIDs, and metadata + before/after snapshots are recursively PHI-sanitized at a single repository choke point. Read is branch-scoped and dentist_owner-only; viewing the log is itself a self-audited security event.

rules.description: Audit events are append-only and immutable — no UPDATE or DELETE, ever. Enforced at TWO layers: (1) HTTP — PATCH/PUT/DELETE /dental/audit-events/:id → 405 AUDIT_EVENT_IMMUTABLE; (2) DB — migration 0080 installs a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE FOR EACH ROW trigger on dental_audit_log that RAISEs 'append-only', so a direct SQL UPDATE/DELETE (compromised credential, errant migration) is also refused (defense-in-depth). There is NO update/delete handler or route for audit rows.

erasure-legal-hold-retention.description: Cross-cutting data-governance layer (WFG-006 / GDPR Art.17 / RA 10173 §34). Three cooperating handler dirs: dental-erasure (right-to-erasure: request→approve/reject anonymize workflow over dental_erasure_request), dental-legalhold (place/list/release holds over dental_legal_hold), retention (env-gated cron that soft-archives/anonymizes records past their dental_retention_policy period). Admin-only HTTP surface (cross-tenant platform-admin scope — a known product decision, not per-branch RBAC). Headline invariant: an ACTIVE legal hold blocks erasure AND excludes the subject from retention. Hard safety invariants live in the engines (anonymize-not-delete, never-touch-audit, dry-run default, soft-archive-over-hard-delete) so a bad policy/caller cannot cause data loss.

rules.description: A subject under an ACTIVE legal hold is NEVER anonymized. approveErasure consults the real dental_legal_hold store (isPersonUnderLegalHold) — OR a reviewer-asserted flag — and on a hold marks the request 'rejected' with legalHoldBlocked=true, touching no subject data; the engine short-circuits before any target runs (audited erasure.blocked_legal_hold). Releasing the hold then allows a fresh request to anonymize. Never bypassable.

rules.description: Approval redacts PII in place and keeps the de-identified row across Person (name→[ERASED] pseudonym, contactInfo nulled), Patient (emergency contact/provider/pharmacy/history/prefs nulled), ConsentForm (signer identity redacted, state kept), and Imaging (DICOM/finding/annotation identifiers nulled). Engine is DRY-RUN by default — real anonymization only on explicit dryRun:false (approval is the opt-in). Idempotent (a re-run anonymizes 0 → noop, still audited). The ONLY hard delete is the physical S3 radiograph object + its storage file row (DATA_GOVERNANCE §3), performed fail-open at handler scope after the anonymization commits.

Yes').

rules.id: AC-RET-001..006 (retention enforcement: dry-run, soft-archive, protected, legal-hold-excluded)
rules.description: The retention engine evaluates enabled dental_retention_policy rows on a daily cron and enforces hard invariants regardless of policy data: DRY-RUN unless RETENTION_ENFORCEMENT_ENABLED='true' AND dryRun:false; the 'audit' target is protected (never read/actioned) and the 'retain' action is refused; 'delete' is DOWNGRADED to soft-archive (no hard-delete path); records whose owning Person is under an active legal hold are excluded (legalHoldExempt policy flag is NEVER a bypass). dental-portal.description: Patient self-service portal (E4 Phase 1: read-only foundation). An EXTERNAL trust boundary: the actor is a PATIENT (Better-Auth system role 'user', linked Person + dental_patient row, NO dental_membership), not clinic staff. Three self-scoped READ endpoints — GET /me/appointments, /me/invoices, /me/balance — each handled under services/api-ts/src/handlers/dental-portal/. Headline invariant: a patient may ONLY ever read their OWN data. There is NO client-supplied patientId on any portal route; identity is derived server-side from the session via resolveSelfPatientIdOrThrow (assert-self-patient.ts), using the codebase invariant user.id === person.id → dental_patient.person_id, eliminating IDOR by construction. Routes gate authMiddleware({roles:['user']}); the staff-vs-patient distinction (a staff-only account with no linked patient → 403) is enforced in-handler, not by role. Read-only: no /me write/pay/mutate routes exist. Guardian/household-dependent access, self-booking, self-pay, secure messaging, consent management, and /me imaging/clinical reads are Phase-2 DEFERRED (not built).

rules.description: Every portal read returns ONLY the session patient's own rows. The patientId is derived server-side from the session (resolveSelfPatientIdOrThrow → patients.person === user.id); no route accepts a client-supplied patientId. A tampered ?patientId=<other> query param is INERT (the handler ignores all client input). Patient A can never read patient B's appointments/invoices/balance.

emr-consultation.description: EMR consultation notes — the upstream Monobase telemedicine consultation-notes module at namespace /emr (table consultation_note), ported from monobase-mycure. A PLATFORM-LEVEL module governed by Better-Auth roles (provider/patient/admin, with provider:owner/patient:owner) — NOT the dental membership/branch matrix and NOT the future external-records-import bridge (/dental/emr-import). Isolation is by PROVIDER/PATIENT OWNERSHIP only: there is NO branch/tenant boundary (tenant_id is nullable and intentionally NOT the isolation mechanism; clinical PHI lives in per-user embedded SQLite and cadence P2P scope claims handle cross-device isolation). Six endpoints under services/api-ts/src/handlers/emr/: createConsultation (POST /emr/consultations), listConsultations (GET), getConsultation (GET /{id}), updateConsultation (PATCH /{id}), finalizeConsultation (POST /{id}/finalize), listEMRPatients (GET /emr/patients). Lifecycle draft → finalized is TERMINAL — no amend-after-finalize (the 'amended' enum value is reserved/unreachable, V-EMR-001). Every PHI create/read/mutation/bulk-read writes a dental_audit_log row via logAuditEvent using dotted-lowercase emr.<resource>.<verb> action names (V-EMR-006).

rules.description: Isolation is by provider/patient ownership resolved server-side from the session — there is no branch/tenant boundary. A provider may read/update/finalize ONLY their own notes; a patient may read ONLY their own notes; admin reads all. getConsultation grants admin OR owning-provider OR owning-patient, else 403. List endpoints force the provider/patient scope from the session (provider sees own, patient sees own, admin sees all) so a provider/patient can never see another owner's rows via the list. A patient passing another patient's id in ?patient= is rejected (403) — scope cannot be widened by a client param.

provider.description: Provider / Practitioner directory — the upstream Monobase platform module at namespace /providers (handlers under services/api-ts/src/handlers/provider/). A PLATFORM-LEVEL, FHIR R4-aligned module governed by Better-Auth roles (user/admin/credentialing/clinician/support, with practitioner:owner) — NOT the dental membership/branch matrix. Three surfaces: (1) Provider — a pre-FHIR self-service profile created by the authenticated user for THEMSELVES (POST /providers, role 'user'; the person is derived server-side, the 'provider' role is granted and the session invalidated; PROVIDER_EXISTS 409 if one already exists). The Provider model carries NO confidential fields (providerType/yearsOfExperience/biography/specialties only). (2) Practitioner — the FHIR Practitioner record carrying CONFIDENTIAL credentials (NPI/DEA/state-license numbers) + qualifications; CRUD is admin/credentialing-managed and reads are admin/clinician/support-gated. There is NO public or patient-facing read of a practitioner, so the credential numbers are never exposed outside clinical/credentialing staff (confidentiality is the ROUTE ROLE GATE, not a handler projection). (3) PractitionerRole — FHIR PractitionerRole (location/availability/role assignment), same admin/credentialing governance. Live surface = 11 codegen-registered ops (createProvider + 5 practitioner ops + 5 practitioner-role ops). NOTE: getProvider/listProviders/updateProvider/deleteProvider handler files exist but are ORPHAN (no TypeSpec op, not in the registry, no route) — the live Provider surface is create-only; provider reads happen via person-expansion and the EMR facade.

rules.id: V-PROV-001 (Provider profile is self-service — created for the session user only)
rules.description: POST /providers creates a provider profile for the AUTHENTICATED user only — the person is derived server-side from the session (demographics are NOT accepted in the body), so a user cannot create a profile for someone else. A second create for the same person → 409 PROVIDER_EXISTS. On success the 'provider' role is granted and the current session is revoked (forces re-auth with the new role).

rules.id: V-PROV-002 (Practitioner credentials are privileged-read-only — no public/patient projection)
rules.description: The FHIR Practitioner record carries confidential credentials (NPI/DEA/state-license numbers) and qualification license identifiers. Every read path (GET /providers/practitioners, GET /providers/practitioners/{id}) is role-gated to admin|clinician|support|practitioner:owner — there is NO public or patient-facing route returning a practitioner. The handler returns the FULL record verbatim (no redaction): confidentiality of the license numbers depends ENTIRELY on the route role gate, never on a handler-level field projection. If a credential-bearing public/patient read is ever added, the projection MUST change to exclude credential.number/qualification.identifier.

rules.id: V-PROV-003 (Practitioner / PractitionerRole writes are admin/credentialing role-gated)
rules.description: Create/update of practitioners and practitioner-roles requires admin|credentialing; deactivate (soft-delete, sets active=false; records retained for audit/legal) requires admin. These role gates are the security boundary (enforced by the route authMiddleware). The route-level practitioner:owner role on get/update is non-gating by design (authMiddleware delegates ownership to the handler) — BUT the practitioner schema has NO person link and the handlers perform NO ownership check, and practitioner:owner is never granted to any user, so it is currently a non-functional/aspirational role (latent: were it ever granted on a bare token it would IDOR-read/update any practitioner — surfaced as spec drift, not exploitable today).

external-records-import.description: External-records / data-import bridge — the surface that ingests OUTSIDE clinical records into the system. Resolved across THREE artifacts: (1) the FUTURE-PHASE FHIR/CDA/PDF EMR-import bridge specced at docs/product/modules/external-records-import/ (namespace /dental/emr-import, table emr_record) — NOT BUILT (MODULE_SPEC §1/§20 declare it Phase 3+, NO handler directory exists); (2) the BUILT single external-PMD ingestion at services/api-ts/src/handlers/dental-pmd/importPMD.ts (POST /dental/pmd — see the dental-pmd block V-PMD-005..008 for imported-PMD immutability/checksum/provenance/audit — the imported original is verbatim + read-only, 405 IMPORTED_PMD_IMMUTABLE on mutate); (3) the BUILT bulk patient-demographics ingestion at services/api-ts/src/handlers/dental-patient/identity/importPatients.ts (POST /dental/patients/import, FR7.2 — CSV/JSON, demographics ONLY per the PRD Phase-1 limitation, no clinical history). This block covers the DISTINCT bulk-import surface (the PMD pieces live in dental-pmd; the FHIR bridge is unbuilt). Bulk import creates NEW person+patient rows (no patient-match/dedup — every row is a fresh record, so there is NO mis-attribution-to-existing-patient surface); cross-tenant isolation + owner-only role gating + all-or-nothing transaction are enforced BEFORE any DB write.

rules.id: V-XRI-001 (bulk patient import is cross-tenant isolated — owner-of-the-named-branch only)
rules.description: POST /dental/patients/import authorizes EVERY unique row.branchId via assertBranchRole(user, branchId, ['dentist_owner']) BEFORE the all-or-nothing transaction. A caller who is not an active dentist_owner of a named branch → 403 ForbiddenError and ZERO rows are written (an import naming another org's branch cannot attach a patient into it). branchId is trusted from the row only insofar as the caller must own it; the facade insertPatientForImport writes preferredBranchId=branchId only after the guard passes. This is the V-PAT-002 lens applied to ingestion: the import cannot exfiltrate into / attach to a branch the caller cannot reach.

rules.id: V-XRI-002 (ingestion safety — untrusted external input rejected with a specific 4xx, never a 500/crash)
rules.description: Bulk import is untrusted external input and is hardened to fail closed with a SPECIFIC 4xx, never an unhandled 500: malformed JSON body → 400 'Invalid JSON body'; non-array / wrong-shape body → 400; empty array / no data rows → 400 (JSON) or 422 (CSV 'no data rows'); any row missing the required firstName or branchId → 422 with per-row error strings (all rows validated up front before any write); a failed commit → 422 'Import transaction failed' (not a 500). Oversized field VALUES are tolerated as ordinary string data (no unbounded-field crash). KNOWN LIMITATION (surfaced, not a defect): there is NO server-side row-COUNT cap — a very large array is parsed fully into memory and committed in one transaction; a future max-rows guard is a product decision (PRD FR7.2 mentions 500+).

rules.id: V-XRI-003 (FHIR/CDA/PDF EMR-import bridge is FUTURE-PHASE — not built)
rules.description: The external-records-import MODULE_SPEC (docs/product/modules/external-records-import/, namespace /dental/emr-import, table emr_record, sources hl7_fhir/cda/pdf/csv) is a Phase-3+ planned boundary with NO handler directory, NO TypeSpec module, and NO routes. Its declared invariants (AC-EMR-001 PATCH/DELETE → 405 EMR_IMMUTABLE; AC-EMR-002 read-only no-auto-merge; AC-EMR-003 source_system required) are ASPIRATIONAL and mirror the live ImportedPMD pattern (BR-022 analog). When built it MUST: bind to patient by UUID with no DB FK (loose coupling), be immutable after import, require source_system, gate import to dentist_owner|dentist_associate, and validate file type/size + reject malformed FHIR/CDA (XXE-safe XML parse, size cap) with a specific 4xx. Surfaced as absent-by-design, NOT a gap.

rules.id: V-XRI-004 (imported-PMD immutability + checksum + provenance + audit are the PMD-side import invariants)
rules.description: Cross-reference — the single-record external-PMD ingestion (importPMD.ts) enforces: imported original stored VERBATIM and read-only (PATCH/PUT/DELETE on /dental/pmd/imported/:id → 405 IMPORTED_PMD_IMMUTABLE, app.ts guard); optional checksum verification (sha256 mismatch → 422 CHECKSUM_MISMATCH); REQUIRED sourceDescription provenance (≤200 chars → 422 VALIDATION_ERROR); branch authorization via patient.preferredBranchId (assertBranchRole dentist_owner|associate|staff_full); and a synchronous dental_audit_log row (action 'pmd.import'). These are fully specced/tested in the dental-pmd block (V-PMD-005..008, imported-pmd-immutable.test.ts, dental-pmd-events.test.ts) — listed here for the import-surface map; NOT re-audited.
